title: Edge Security, WAF, Hotlinking, Headers, and Failure Containment · Execution Trace: Geo Privacy Boundary
summary: A public distribution trusts Referer as authorization, caches private errors, lacks browser security headers, and forwards bot bursts to an expensive origin. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the geo privacy boundary increment in layered edge-security and abuse-control plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use geographic restrictions only with documented business, legal, accuracy, accessibility, VPN, and privacy limits.
Set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Geo Privacy Boundary · Theory Geo Privacy Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 8922 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to layer application authorization, WAF, origin protection, headers, and safe error caching; preserve accessible progress, cancellation, status, and recovery evidence; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Geo Privacy Boundary contract for the supplied data.
